children.heading: Delayed entry into alarm monitoring
children.label: a Delayed entry into alarm monitoring

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 1 Prohibition
children.text: No Bell operating company or affiliate thereof shall engage in the provision of alarm monitoring services before the date which is 5 years after .February 8, 1996

children.heading: Existing activities
children.label: 2 Existing activities
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not prohibit or limit the provision, directly or through an affiliate, of alarm monitoring services by a Bell operating company that was engaged in providing alarm monitoring services as of , directly or through an affiliate. Such Bell operating company or affiliate may not acquire any equity interest in, or obtain financial control of, any unaffiliated alarm monitoring service entity after , and until 5 years after , except that this sentence shall not prohibit an exchange of customers for the customers of an unaffiliated alarm monitoring service entity.November 30, 1995November 30, 1995February 8, 1996
provide nonaffiliated entities, upon reasonable request, with the network services it provides to its own alarm monitoring operations, on nondiscriminatory terms and conditions; and
not subsidize its alarm monitoring services either directly or indirectly from telephone exchange service operations.

children.heading: Expedited consideration of complaints
children.label: c Expedited consideration of complaints
children.text: The Commission shall establish procedures for the receipt and review of complaints concerning violations of subsection (b) or the regulations thereunder that result in material financial harm to a provider of alarm monitoring service. Such procedures shall ensure that the Commission will make a final determination with respect to any such complaint within 120 days after receipt of the complaint. If the complaint contains an appropriate showing that the alleged violation occurred, as determined by the Commission in accordance with such regulations, the Commission shall, within 60 days after receipt of the complaint, order the incumbent local exchange carrier (as defined in ) and its affiliates to cease engaging in such violation pending such final determination.section 251(h) of this title

children.heading: Use of data
children.label: d Use of data
children.text: A local exchange carrier may not record or use in any fashion the occurrence or contents of calls received by providers of alarm monitoring services for the purposes of marketing such services on behalf of such local exchange carrier, or any other entity. Any regulations necessary to enforce this subsection shall be issued initially within 6 months after .February 8, 1996

to receive signals from other devices located at or about such premises regarding a possible threat at such premises to life, safety, or property, from burglary, fire, vandalism, bodily injury, or other emergency, and

to transmit a signal regarding such threat by means of transmission facilities of a local exchange carrier or one of its affiliates to a remote monitoring center to alert a person at such center of the need to inform the customer or another person or police, fire, rescue, security, or public safety personnel of such threat,
